name: Caretaker Government of Lebanon
title: Caretaker Prime Minister of the Lebanese Republic

compatibilityOverrides.reason: Hezbollah's Iranian proxy status dominates Lebanese security policy
Saudi Arabia withdrew support over Hezbollah influence
Regional Sunni solidarity and shared concern over Iranian expansion

biography: Lebanon's post-crisis caretaker government represents the collapse of the country's confessional power-sharing system. Following the 2020 Beirut port explosion, hyperinflation that destroyed the currency, and a banking sector meltdown, the state functions as little more than a shell. The ISFP-type leadership reflects a weak, reactive caretaker apparatus unable to form a permanent government, caught between Hezbollah's parallel military state, sectarian warlords, and an international community demanding reforms. Lebanon's once-vibrant economy has shrunk by over 60%, and the country serves as a cautionary tale of state failure in the modern era.
